answers.answer: B) Archival meant different things to each team - product likely expected data export + purge for compliance, while engineering interpreted it as soft-delete for potential reactivation. Reason: This is a classic terminology ambiguity problem. Archival has different meanings in different contexts - compliance archival (export then purge) vs operational archival (soft-delete for recovery). Solution: Define terms clearly in requirements phase, establish shared vocabulary.

Top 5 Failure Modes by probability: 1) Redis single node failure - no replica, causes session loss and cache stampede to DB. Mitigation: Enable Redis Cluster or master-replica. 2) RDS connection pool exhaustion - 60 connections insufficient under high load, causes timeouts. Mitigation: Use RDS Proxy, increase pool size. 3) Single-region dependency (us-east-1) - region outage takes down entire system. Mitigation: Multi-region deployment or DR plan. 4) No circuit breakers for external APIs - Stripe/SendGrid failures block threads and exhaust resources. Mitigation: Implement circuit breaker pattern with timeouts and retry limits. 5) CloudWatch 5-minute intervals too long - delayed fault detection and response. Mitigation: Reduce to 1-minute intervals, add real-time alerting.
